verses.text: He that is wounded in the stones, or hath his privy member cut off, shall not enter into the congregation of the LORD. A bastard shall not enter; even to his tenth generation shall he not enter. Guidance: A startling exclusion list. Mutilated men and children born outside lawful marriage were barred from the worshipping assembly — a sign that holiness ran through bodily integrity and family origin in covenant Israel. Isaiah 56 will later reverse it: eunuchs who choose the LORD get a name better than sons. The law's hard edge gets softened by prophecy that follows.

Guidance: A startling exclusion list. Mutilated men and children born outside lawful marriage were barred from the worshipping assembly — a sign that holiness ran through bodily integrity and family origin in covenant Israel. Isaiah 56 will later reverse it: eunuchs who choose the LORD get a name better than sons. The law's hard edge gets softened by prophecy that follows.

An Ammonite or Moabite shall not enter into the congregation of the LORD; even to their tenth generation, for ever: because they met you not with bread and water; because they hired Balaam to curse thee. Nevertheless the LORD turned the curse into a blessing, because the LORD thy God loved thee. Thou shalt not seek their peace all thy days. Guidance: A long memory. Ammon and Moab denied bread; that crime echoes for ten generations. But notice the parenthesis: He turned the curse into a blessing, because He loved you. Even when the world hires curses, God's love stays in the rewrite room. (Ruth the Moabite later marries into Boaz's line — exception by individual faith.)

Thou shalt not abhor an Edomite; he is thy brother: thou shalt not abhor an Egyptian; because thou wast a stranger in his land. The children that are begotten of them shall enter into the congregation of the LORD in their third generation. Guidance: Edom is family; Egypt was the home before the bondage. Don't despise them. Even oppressors get the third-generation door. God's covenant is not a permanent rejection — it remembers brotherhood AND it remembers shelter. Past pain does not extinguish present mercy.

When the host goeth forth, keep thee from every wicked thing. If a man be not clean by an uncleanness that chanceth him by night, he shall go abroad out of the camp; at sun-down, wash and come in. Have a place without the camp; have a paddle upon thy weapon; dig, cover that which cometh from thee: for the LORD thy God walketh in the midst of thy camp; therefore shall thy camp be holy. Guidance: God walks among the soldiers. Therefore the latrines are outside the wall and the paddle is on every belt. The most striking detail in ancient warfare codes: hygiene is theology here. Holiness comes down to whether you covered up.

Thou shalt not deliver unto his master the servant which is escaped from his master unto thee: he shall dwell with thee in that place which he shall choose, in one of thy gates, where it liketh him best: thou shalt not oppress him. Guidance: An astonishing law in the ancient world. Israel did not extradite runaway slaves — the runaway chose where to live. The escaped person had agency, not chains. Israel's identity as a freed-slave people made the harbor obligatory. God's rescue ripples outward.

There shall be no whore of the daughters of Israel, nor a sodomite of the sons of Israel. Thou shalt not bring the hire of a whore, or the price of a dog, into the house of the LORD thy God for any vow: even both these are abomination. Guidance: Cult prostitution — sacred sexuality at pagan shrines — was banned cold. And no money earned from prostitution could be donated to the temple. God refuses laundered offerings. The altar is not a way to clean dirty money.

There shall be no whore of the daughters of Israel, nor a sodomite of the sons of Israel. Thou shalt not bring the hire of a whore, or the price of a dog, into the house of the LORD thy God for any vow: even both these are abomination. Guidance: Cult prostitution — sacred sexuality at pagan shrines — was banned cold. And no money earned from prostitution could be donated to the temple. God refuses laundered offerings. The altar is not a way to clean dirty money.

Thou shalt not lend upon usury to thy brother — usury of money, victuals, any thing lent upon usury: unto a stranger thou mayest lend upon usury; but unto thy brother thou shalt not lend upon usury. Guidance: Banking inside the covenant family had no interest rate. A loan to a brother was a help, not an investment. The foreign loan could carry interest because it was a transaction; the brother loan was relational. Money among God's people moves differently than money in the market.

Thou shalt not lend upon usury to thy brother — usury of money, victuals, any thing lent upon usury: unto a stranger thou mayest lend upon usury; but unto thy brother thou shalt not lend upon usury. The foreign loan could carry interest because it was a transaction; the brother loan was relational. Money among God's people moves differently than money in the market.

When thou shalt vow a vow unto the LORD, thou shalt not slack to pay it: for the LORD thy God will surely require it of thee; it would be sin in thee. But if thou shalt forbear to vow, it shall be no sin. That which is gone out of thy lips thou shalt keep and perform. Guidance: No one is required to make a vow. But once made, it is fixed. God doesn't require oaths — He requires honored words. The freedom is in the silence before the vow; the burden is in the vow once said.

When thou comest into thy neighbour's vineyard, thou mayest eat grapes thy fill; but thou shalt not put any in thy vessel. When thou comest into the standing corn of thy neighbour, thou mayest pluck the ears with thine hand; but thou shalt not move a sickle unto thy neighbour's standing corn. Guidance: A hungry traveler could pick grapes and snap heads of wheat as he walked — but couldn't load a basket. The fields were a shared larder, not a stockyard. God writes hospitality into property. Jesus' disciples would later eat exactly this way on a sabbath, and He would defend them by this verse.
